scope.summary: Implement a unified Tailor Library browser panel (operator and model accessible) listing local garments, fabric presets, trim catalog, and starter templates by category with search and stable element IDs
acceptance_criteria: GET /tailor/library/browse accepts ?kind=garment|preset|trim|template and ?q= search string and returns a structured list with stable IDs, name, kind, preview_ref, and status
The frontend LibraryBrowserPanel renders four tabs (Garments, Fabrics, Trims, Templates) populated from the browse endpoint
Every item in the panel has a stable data-testid element ID for parallel-model interaction (HBR-VIS)
A model can call tailor_library_browse MCP tool with kind filter and receive the same structured list without a GUI session
Selecting a garment from the panel loads it into the active GarmentSpec authoring context
Selecting a preset from the panel assigns it to the currently selected panel in the authoring view
The panel renders correctly in the capture matrix (MT-244) including in the empty and populated states
No window focus change occurs when the panel is opened or a library item is selected (HBR-QUIET compliance)
proof_targets: HBR-VIS: headless capture of LibraryBrowserPanel in all four tab states passes screenshot gate
HBR-QUIET: opening the panel produces no foreground window or focus change
scope.risk_if_missed: Without a browsable library panel, operators and models cannot discover or reuse local assets without constructing ID-based API queries by hand; the gap degrades the authoring workflow to a code-only experience and eliminates the operator-visible library surface that MD users expect as the entry point for garment reuse.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
